title: Gynochthodes suratmanii
taxonomy: Gynochthodes suratmanii belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Gynochthodes. This species is classified as a member of the clade Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. Gynochthodes suratmanii is native to the tropical regions of Asia, and its classification is closely related to other species in the same genus. intro: Gynochthodes suratmanii, also known as the Suratman's Gynochthodes, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate conditions such as fever, wounds, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gynochthodes suratmanii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions, making it a valuable resource in natural healing practices.
